request.question: The son of Stuart James McCullum is an international cricketer who represents which country?

title: Kyle Jarvis
Tudor architecture
Stu Roberts
Stuart Karppinen
Stuart Davidson (cricketer)

sentences: Kyle Malcolm Jarvis (born 16 February 1989) is a Zimbabwean cricketer who represents Zimbabwe and played for Lancashire.
The son of another former Zimbabwean international cricketer Malcolm Jarvis, he was educated at St Johns College, Harare, where he excelled at rugby and cricket.
Kyle was a key bowler for Zimbabwe in the 2008 Under-19 World Cup and he also played for the Zimbabwe U-19 Rugby Team.

The Tudor architectural style is the final development of Medieval architecture in England, during the Tudor period (1485–1603) and even beyond, and also the tentative introduction of Renaissance architecture to England.
It is generally not used to refer to the whole period of the Tudor dynasty (1485-1603), but in prestige buildings to the period roughly between 1500 and 1560.
It followed the Late Gothic Perpendicular style and was superseded by Elizabethan architecture from about 1560 in domestic building of any pretensions to fashion.

In the much more slow-moving styles of vernacular architecture "Tudor" has become a designation for styles like half-timbering that characterise the few buildings surviving from before 1485 and others from the Stuart period.
In this form the Tudor style long retained its hold on English taste.

Nevertheless, 'Tudor style' is an awkward style-designation, with its implied suggestions of continuity through the period of the Tudor dynasty and the misleading impression that there was a style break at the accession of Stuart James I in 1603.
Stuart James Roberts (born 22 March 1965 in Christchurch) is a former New Zealand cricketer who played two One Day Internationals in 1990.
Stuart James Karppinen (born 13 June 1973) is a former Australian cricketer who is the current strength and conditioning coach of the Australian national cricket team.
Born in Townsville, Karppinen moved to Canberra with his family at a young age.
He played for the Australian Capital Territory Under-19s at the 1991–92 Barclays Bank Australian Under-19 Championships, taking one wicket in his only match.

He was first selected for the Australian Capital Territory senior side during the 1993–94 season, although the ACT did not at the time have either first-class or List A status, and thus only played state teams' Second XIs and other sides touring Canberra.
Karppinen was also the recipient of an Australian Institute of Sport scholarship in 1997.
The Australian Capital Territory was admitted to the Australian domestic limited-overs competition for the 1997–98 season, playing as the "Canberra Comets".
Karppinen played six matches for the team in their inaugural season, taking nine wickets with a best of 2/35.
Karppinen did not play any matches the following season, and transferred to Western Australia for the 1999–2000 season.
Stuart James Davidson (born 8 March 1972) is a Zimbabwean-born former Scottish cricketer.
Davidson was a right-handed batsman who bowled right-arm medium pace.
Devika Palshikar (Devanagari:देविका पळशीकर, born 20 June 1979 in Malvan, Maharashtra, India) is a Test and One Day International cricketer who represents India.
She has played one Test and 15 ODIs and represents Maharashtra and West Zone in India's domestic league.
Stuart James Airey (born 18 March 1983) is an English cricketer.
Airey is right-handed batsman who bowled right-arm medium-fast.
He was born in Cleethorpes, Lincolnshire.
Stuart James McCullum (born 6 December 1956) is a former cricketer who played for Otago throughout his career.
A left-handed opening batsman who occasionally kept-wicket, he is the father of New Zealand international players Brendon and Nathan McCullum.
Nathan Leslie McCullum (born 1 September 1980) is a former New Zealand international cricketer, who represented New Zealand in Twenty20 Internationals and One Day Internationals.

Brendon Barrie McCullum {'1': ", '2': ", '3': ", '4': "} (born 27 September 1981), popularly Brendon "Baz" McCullum is a former New Zealand international cricketer, who played all formats and also a former captain in all forms.
A big hitting legend in limited over cricket, McCullum took quick scoring to Test matches as well, notably recording the fastest test century of all time.
He is considered as one of the most successful captains of New Zealand cricket.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: The son of Stuart James McCullum is an international cricketer who represents which country?
hop1_docs: Stuart McCullum | Stuart James McCullum (born 6 December 1956) is a former cricketer who played for Otago throughout his career.  A left-handed opening batsman who occasionally kept-wicket, he is the father of New Zealand international players Brendon and Nathan McCullum.
Nathan McCullum | Nathan Leslie McCullum (born 1 September 1980) is a former New Zealand international cricketer, who represented New Zealand in Twenty20 Internationals and One Day Internationals.

Brendon McCullum | Brendon Barrie McCullum {'1': ", '2': ", '3': ", '4': "} (born 27 September 1981), popularly Brendon "Baz" McCullum is a former New Zealand international cricketer, who played all formats and also a former captain in all forms.  A big hitting legend in limited over cricket, McCullum took quick scoring to Test matches as well, notably recording the fastest test century of all time.  He is considered as one of the most successful captains of New Zealand cricket.

New Zealand cricket team in South Africa in 2015 | The New Zealand cricket team toured South Africa from 14 to 26 August 2015.  The tour consisted of three One Day International and two Twenty20 International matches.  In June Brendon McCullum was named as the captain for New Zealand on this tour.  However, in July, it was announced that McCullum had been rested for this tour and the tour to Zimbabwe, being replaced by Kane Williamson.

Stuart Kennedy (cricketer) | Stuart Robert Kennedy (born 9 January 1965) is a former Scottish international cricketer who represented the Scottish national side between 1995 and 1997.  He played as a right-arm medium-pace bowler.

Gareth Hopkins | Gareth James Hopkins (born 24 November 1976 in Lower Hutt) is a former New Zealand cricketer.  A wicket-keeper, he played five One Day Internationals in 2004 after Brendon McCullum went home from England for the NatWest series.

Lahore Qalandars | Lahore Qalandars (Urdu: ‎ ; Punjabi: ; acronym LQ) is a Pakistani professional cricket franchise which plays in the Pakistan Super League. The Home ground of the team is Gaddafi Stadium. It nominally represents the city of Lahore and the province Punjab in the league.  The team is owned by Qatar Lubricants Company Limited (QALCO). The team is currently captained by Brendon McCullum and coached by Paddy Upton, a former South African cricketer.  Lahore Qalandars was the second most expensive franchise and was one of the few teams sold to an international company.
hop1_titles: New Zealand cricket team in South Africa in 2015

They are one of the five teams that had a competition in the 2017 Pakistan Super League.  The team was captained by Brendon McCullum, and they won three matches from their eight matches in the PSL 2017.  They stand on fifth position and as a result they were eliminated in group stage.

New Zealand cricket team in South Africa in 2015 | The New Zealand cricket team toured South Africa from 14 to 26 August 2015.  The tour consisted of three One Day International and two Twenty20 International matches.  In June Brendon McCullum was named as the captain for New Zealand on this tour.  However, in July, it was announced that McCullum had been rested for this tour and the tour to Zimbabwe, being replaced by Kane Williamson.

The team is currently captained by Brendon McCullum and coached by Paddy Upton, a former South African cricketer. CricHQ | CricHQ is a digital platform for sport which combines competition management and administration software with live scoring and statistics for cricket clubs.  It is based in Wellington, New Zealand, and was set up by CEO Simon Baker and former New Zealand cricketers Stephen Fleming and Brendon McCullum.  It manages the administration of cricket test countries New Zealand, Sri Lanka, South Africa and Zimbabwe.  A number of other national governing bodies also use its services from club level upwards (see National Governing Bodies section below).
